dialogue.title: Trustworthy Research Assistant With Verified Citations design review
dialogue.review_notice: This is a simulated design critique for an architecture graph. It is not a live execution, not proof of correctness, and does not call external systems.

turns.speaker: Builder
turns.text: The retrieval to grounded_prompt to answer_generator path is the right early spine because the task requires citations and high privacy sensitivity. It keeps generation tied to retrieved evidence instead of letting the model answer from memory alone.

turns.speaker: Reviewer
turns.text: The risk is that answer_generator followed by citation_verifier may create a slow path before the user sees value. The validation issue points to latency around generation and verification, so the design needs a clear minimum path for the first demo.

turns.speaker: Builder
turns.text: The citation_verifier and human_review nodes reduce trust risk by adding checks after the generated answer. For a research assistant, that review layer is important because verified citations are part of the core task profile, not an optional polish step.

turns.speaker: Reviewer
turns.text: There is a tradeoff between reliability and latency: human_review improves confidence, but it may make the system feel heavy for simple research questions. The graph should make it clear when review is mandatory versus when a verified draft can be shown earlier.

turns.speaker: Mentor
turns.text: A citation-heavy workflow should separate evidence grounding, answer generation, and verification so each trust boundary is visible. The latency issue shows that trust checks need to be designed as part of the user experience, not added only at the end.

turns.speaker: Reviewer
turns.text: For the MVP, keep one linear path: retrieval, grounded_prompt, answer_generator, citation_verifier, human_review, and final_answer. Defer broader retrieval expansion, repeated verification loops, or extra review stages until the latency risk is understood.

lessons.title: Evidence First
lessons.text: The graph teaches that research answers should begin with retrieval and a grounded prompt before generation. This keeps the answer path aligned with the citation requirement.

lessons.title: Balance trust and latency
lessons.text: Citation verification and human review improve reliability, but they also define the slowest part of the experience. The MVP should keep these trust gates while limiting unnecessary expansion around them.
